recent_calls.summary: The user called Budget and was prompted multiple times to provide their five-digit service address ZIP code but only pressed 0 repeatedly. The user then requested to speak with a live agent, and the call was successfully transferred to a live agent for further assistance.
The agent attempted to engage the user by requesting their service address ZIP code, but the user did not respond and ended the call shortly after.

The user called to inquire about a scheduled appointment for the day, expressing concern that the technician had not arrived by the expected time. The agent confirmed the appointment details and attempted to transfer the call to a live agent for further assistance. The call ended with a successful transfer to another agent.
The user, William, was unable to communicate clearly and decided to call back the next day. The agent, Sophie, offered assistance and left the conversation open for future contact.

The user, Paula Caldwell, called to verify the legitimacy of a suspicious letter regarding a home warranty allegedly secured by GoodLeap. The agent acknowledged the concern, explained they couldn't access billing details, and transferred the call to the billing team for further assistance. The user provided contact information and expressed worry about potential fraud.
The user requested to speak with a customer service representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was completed successfully without any issues.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent acknowledged the request and completed the transfer without issue.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, acknowledged the request and completed a successful call transfer.

Steve called to reschedule his appointment from Friday to Monday for an evaporator replacement on his cooling system. The agent confirmed the details and informed Steve that the request would be passed to the team for confirmation. The call ended with the agent asking for feedback.

Crystal called to schedule a home warranty service appointment for an electrical issue with her air conditioner at 447 Terry Drive, Columbia, Illinois. The agent confirmed details, including the home warranty dispatch number, and Crystal declined information about maintenance programs. She provided positive feedback, rating the experience as a 5 for its quickness.
The user, Pamela, requested to speak with a live agent during the call. The initial agent, Sophie, acknowledged the request and transferred the call to another agent for further assistance.
The user requested to speak to a live agent, and the initial agent transferred the call to another representative successfully. The call ended shortly after the transfer was completed.

The user called to inquire about an inspection scheduled between 1:00 and 2:30 at 1204 Iron Street. The agent asked for more details about the type of service for the inspection, but the call ended abruptly before the user could respond.
The user called and requested to speak with Nicole. The agent attempted to connect the user and successfully transferred the call to the appropriate agent.

Haley called to reschedule her air conditioner inspection appointment to Thursday at 1 PM at 1204 Iron Street. The agent confirmed the details and assured Haley that the team would reach out to confirm the new appointment. Haley was satisfied and ended the call politely.

Michael from American Home Shield called to cancel a work order for a tune-up for homeowner Blair Flowers. The agent confirmed the details and received positive feedback from Michael about the fast service. The call ended successfully with no further requests.
The user requested a return call and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance. The transfer was successful and the call ended shortly after.

Wayne from American Home Shield called to follow up on the status of a second opinion air conditioning system work order that was marked completed without a diagnosis. The agent confirmed details and informed Wayne that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact him shortly. Wayne declined information about maintenance programs and did not provide an email for follow-up.
The user requested to speak to a live agent, and the initial agent transferred the call to another agent successfully. The call lasted 16 seconds and ended with a call transfer.

The user called to discuss an estimate and requested to speak to a human agent. The initial agent transferred the call to a live agent to assist further. The call was successfully transferred without any issues.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the call was promptly transferred to another agent for further assistance.

Loretta called to reschedule her air conditioning service appointment originally scheduled for today, as she was stuck at work. The agent confirmed the details and informed her that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would follow up to confirm the new appointment time. Loretta declined information about maintenance programs and provided positive feedback about the interaction, though she expected to speak to a human.

The user, Sierra Johnson, called to inquire about the arrival time of technician Patrick. The agent confirmed the appointment details, informing Sierra that Patrick is scheduled to arrive between 9 AM and 10 AM. The user had no further requests and ended the call.

The user, Patricia, called to schedule a routine air conditioner tune-up through American Home Shield. The agent confirmed the appointment details and the user's role as the homeowner, and Patricia declined additional information about maintenance programs and feedback requests. The call ended with the agent confirming that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would follow up to confirm the appointment.

The user called back after missing a call earlier and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, handled the call efficiently by initiating a transfer to the appropriate representative.

The user, Jim Brandt, called to check on his appointment. The agent confirmed the appointment details scheduled for May 4th with technician Vernon and then transferred the call to a live agent as requested by the user.

The user called to inquire about the timing of a technician visit for a cooling system repair, as they had not received an update. The agent confirmed the service details, noted the user's preference for a morning appointment, and assured that someone would contact them soon to confirm the timing. The user declined information about maintenance programs and ended the call politely.

The user called Budget's after-hours assistant and provided their ZIP code. They requested to speak with Jeff but did not receive a response before ending the call. The call ended abruptly without further interaction.

The call was initiated by the user but ended abruptly without any interaction or information provided. The agent requested the ZIP code for the service address, but the user did not respond before hanging up.

The user called to confirm if a technician was still scheduled to arrive this evening. The agent explained that confirmation couldn't be provided after hours but assured the user's request would be prioritized and someone would contact them soon. The agent also asked for the type of service and a description of the issue.
The call was very brief with the user only saying "C g" and the agent greeting the user twice. No meaningful conversation or assistance took place before the user hung up.

The user called after hours to inquire about the time of their appointment scheduled for tomorrow. The agent informed the user that they could not access the live schedule and advised that the team would confirm the appointment details when they returned at 8 AM. The call ended politely with no further requests from the user.

The user, Manikandan, called after hours requesting to speak with Gigi about a code "Egypt" related to their heating and cooling system. The agent documented the request and informed the user that someone from the team will call back on the next business day. The call ended with no further requests from the user.

Barbara Bennett called to schedule a service appointment for her heat pump, which is not cooling again despite previous repairs. She requested a technician visit tomorrow around 11 or 12 o'clock and expressed concern about recurring issues over two years. The agent confirmed the details and the appointment was successfully scheduled.

Tracey called to return CJ's phone call and requested to set up an appointment with a callback as soon as possible. The agent informed her that since it was after hours, CJ or someone from the team would call her back the next business day to arrange the appointment. The call ended with no further assistance needed.

The user called to cancel and reschedule an air conditioning inspection appointment due to a work conflict. They requested to reschedule the appointment to Thursday at 1 PM and confirmed it was a routine inspection. The agent noted the details and planned to have the team follow up to confirm the new appointment time.

The user, Haley, called after hours to reschedule an appointment to Thursday at 1 PM regarding the circuit breaker box and to cancel an inspection scheduled for tomorrow. The agent documented the requests and informed Haley that a team member would follow up on the next business day. The call ended with no further requests from Haley.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and no further interaction before the user hung up.
The agent greeted the user and asked for the service address ZIP code, but the user hung up shortly after without responding.

The user informed the agent about the scheduling details for furnace service on the thirteenth and mentioned her daughter would be present but arriving late. The user requested to speak with a live person, and the agent transferred the call accordingly.
The user called and requested to speak with Nicole. The agent attempted to connect the user and successfully transferred the call to another agent.

The user, Keith Starry, scheduled routine preventative maintenance for two air conditioners at his home in Weldon Spring, MO, for Thursday afternoon at 12 PM. The agent confirmed the booking details, addressed the user's feedback positively, and completed the appointment booking successfully.

The user, Hannah, called to report an issue with her air conditioning not working and not responding to the thermostat. The agent confirmed the dispatch number, updated the user's phone number and service address, and noted the preference for texting. The user declined additional information about maintenance programs and provided positive feedback on the call experience.

Anne from Home Shield called on behalf of a member to inquire about a canceled cooling system recall. The agent confirmed the details, including the service address and contact information, and scheduled the soonest available appointment. The user provided positive feedback about the call experience.
The call was very brief and ended abruptly after the agent's initial greeting and request for the user's ZIP code. The user hung up without responding.

Jeff called to schedule an emergency service appointment for his broken heating and cooling systems covered by American Home Shield. The agent confirmed the dispatch number, service address, and contact information, and Jeff declined to provide feedback. The call ended with the agent confirming all details and no further action requested by Jeff.

The user called to request repair service for their air conditioner, which is not cooling. The agent collected the reference number and transferred the call to a live representative for further assistance.

The user called to dispatch a service request for a gas furnace and water heater issue and sought confirmation about a canceled plumbing work order. The agent noted the details and assured a callback for clarification. The user declined information about maintenance programs and provided positive feedback on the interaction.
